disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Abrupt-onset, perfectly regular, very rapid palpitations (~185 bpm) starting at rest, like a switch being flipped
trigger_question_examples: How did the racing start - suddenly or gradually?
Is it regular or irregular, and how fast?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: It started completely suddenly while I was sitting watching TV, like a switch flipped - it's a fast, regular machine-gun pounding, and when I count my pulse it's about 185.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Episode sustained for about 45 minutes without self-terminating
trigger_question_examples: How long has this one lasted?
Has it stopped and started, or been continuous?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: It's been going for about three quarters of an hour now and won't stop.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Lightheadedness/near-syncope on standing, mild exertional breathlessness, and a pounding sensation in the neck during the tachycardia
trigger_question_examples: Do you feel dizzy or short of breath with it?
Any feeling you might pass out, especially when you stand?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: Yes, I feel very dizzy and nearly pass out when I stand, I get a bit breathless when I move, and I can feel a strong pounding in my neck.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: No crushing central chest pain and no syncope (currently haemodynamically stable, alert and conversing)
trigger_question_examples: Any severe or crushing chest pain, or have you actually passed out?
Do you feel faint to the point of collapsing?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No crushing chest pain - my chest feels tight but not crushing - and I haven't actually passed out, just the racing and dizziness.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Heavy caffeine intake today (two double espressos); no illicit stimulant use; no family history of sudden cardiac death
trigger_question_examples: Any caffeine, energy drinks, or stimulants today?
Any family history of heart problems or sudden death?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: I had two double espressos today, but no drugs. No one in my family has died suddenly of a heart problem.

patient_initiated_exchanges.patient_text: Is there anything I can do right now to stop it?
patient_initiated_exchanges.expected_ai_doctor_response_quality: Should coach a safe vagal manoeuvre (e.g. a modified Valsalva - bearing down while lying, then legs raised, or blowing hard against a closed airway) as a first-line attempt to terminate SVT in a stable patient, while explaining that if it doesn't work he needs urgent in-person assessment for a heart tracing and possibly a medication to reset the rhythm.

patient_initiated_exchanges.patient_text: If the breathing exercise doesn't work, can I just drive myself to the hospital?
patient_initiated_exchanges.expected_ai_doctor_response_quality: Should advise NOT to drive himself while symptomatic (near-fainting on standing risks collapse at the wheel), to arrange a friend or assisted transport, and to call 000 if he develops severe chest pain, worsening breathlessness or feels he might faint.

deflection_behaviours.description: Patient may want to wait it out or drive himself - tests whether the AI Doctor coaches vagal manoeuvres, assesses stability, and arranges safe urgent assessment.
deflection_behaviours.deflection_text_template: Maybe it'll just stop on its own if I sit quietly - should I wait a bit longer?
